permutations-combinations.name: Problem 1.6.2 (Mastering AMC 8)
permutations-combinations.statement: How many 3-digit numbers exist with all odd digits?

permutations-combinations.name: Problem 1.6.3 (Mastering AMC 8)
permutations-combinations.statement: How many 3-digit numbers exist with all even digits?

permutations-combinations.name: Problem 1.6.4 (Mastering AMC 8)
permutations-combinations.statement: How many 3-digit odd numbers are there with distinct digits?

permutations-combinations.name: Problem 1.6.5 (Mastering AMC 8)
permutations-combinations.statement: If you have Alice, Betty, and Chase and you need to choose two of them to be the president and vice president of the math club, how many ways are there to do this?

permutations-combinations.name: Problem 1.6.6 (Mastering AMC 8)
permutations-combinations.statement: Find the number of ways of selecting a president, vice president, and secretary from a group of 8 people.

permutations-combinations.name: Problem 1.6.7 (Mastering AMC 8)
permutations-combinations.statement: Find the number of 3-digit numbers with all of its digits distinct.

permutations-combinations.name: Problem 1.6.8 (Mastering AMC 8)
permutations-combinations.statement: Find the number of ways to arrange 5 different books on a bookshelf.

permutations-combinations.name: Problem 1.6.9 (Mastering AMC 8)
permutations-combinations.statement: How many ways are there to pick your favorite and second favorite book amongst 5 books?

permutations-combinations.name: Problem 1.6.10 (Mastering AMC 8)
permutations-combinations.statement: There are 10 students at a math contest. How many ways are there to select the first, second, and third place winners?

permutations-combinations.name: Problem 1.6.11 (Mastering AMC 8)
permutations-combinations.statement: How many 4 digit numbers are not palindromes? A palindrome is a number that reads the same forward and backward.

permutations-combinations.name: Problem 1.6.13 (Mastering AMC 8)
permutations-combinations.statement: There is a school race with 12 students. How many different ways to award the gold, silver, and bronze medals if no ties are possible?

permutations-combinations.name: Problem 2.4.1 (Mastering AMC 8)
permutations-combinations.statement: If you have Alice, Betty, Chase, and Dave and you need to choose two of them to be the leaders of the math club, how many ways are there to do this?

permutations-combinations.name: Problem 2.4.2 (Mastering AMC 8)
permutations-combinations.statement: Evaluate ( 8 4 ) and ( 9 7 )

permutations-combinations.name: Problem 2.4.3 (Mastering AMC 8)
permutations-combinations.statement: How many ways are there to select 3 fruits amongst 8 different fruits?

permutations-combinations.name: Problem 2.4.4 (Mastering AMC 8)
permutations-combinations.statement: How many ways are there to choose 4 balls from a bag which contains 9 balls of dif- ferent color?

permutations-combinations.name: Problem 2.4.5 (Mastering AMC 8)
permutations-combinations.statement: Mrs. Jones is organizing a potluck and wants to serve 8 different dishes. Each fam- ily can bring 3 dishes. How many families can Mrs. Jones invite so that no two families bring the same combination of dishes? 36

permutations-combinations.name: Problem 2.4.6 (Mastering AMC 8)
permutations-combinations.statement: There are 9 different flavors of ice cream at an ice cream shop. Joe wants to buy 2 or 3 scoops of different ice cream flavors. He doesn’t like 2 of the flavors Mango and Chocolate together so won’t buy them together . How many ways can he buy ice cr

permutations-combinations.name: Problem 2.4.10 (Mastering AMC 8)
permutations-combinations.statement: How many 5 digit positive integers exist such that the third digit is 8, the first 3 digits ascending, and last 3 digits are descending?

permutations-combinations.name: Problem 3.3.1 (Mastering AMC 8)
permutations-combinations.statement: How many ways are there to arrange the letters of the word MATH?

permutations-combinations.name: Problem 3.3.5 (Mastering AMC 8)
permutations-combinations.statement: How many arrangements of the word BOTTLE are there such that the 2 T’s and O remain together?

permutations-combinations.name: Problem 3.3.7 (Mastering AMC 8)
permutations-combinations.statement: You have 6 apples, 3 pears, and 2 oranges. Assuming the same type of fruits are in- distinguishable, how many distinct ways are there to place the fruits in a line if the 2 oranges must be next to each other?

intro-probability.name: Problem 4.7.1 (Mastering AMC 8)
intro-probability.statement: Two 6-sided dice are rolled. What is the probability that the sum of the numbers rolled is 2?

intro-probability.name: Problem 4.7.2 (Mastering AMC 8)
intro-probability.statement: Two 6-sided dice are rolled. What is the probability that the sum of the numbers rolled is 4?

intro-probability.name: Problem 4.7.3 (Mastering AMC 8)
intro-probability.statement: Two dice are rolled. What is the probability that the sum of the numbers rolled is 6?

systematic-casework.name: Problem 5.3.3 (Mastering AMC 8)
systematic-casework.statement: How many 3-digit even numbers are there with distinct digits?

systematic-casework.name: Problem 5.3.20 (Mastering AMC 8)
systematic-casework.statement: Three unique fair tetrahedral die, whose faces are numbered 1, 2, 3, and 4 are rolled. How many ways can the sum of the numbers rolled be 7?

complementary-counting.name: Problem 6.3.1 (Mastering AMC 8)
complementary-counting.statement: If you toss four coins, in how many ways will there be at least 1 head?

complementary-counting.name: Problem 6.3.2 (Mastering AMC 8)
complementary-counting.statement: If you toss four coins, in how many ways will there be at least 2 heads?

complementary-counting.name: Problem 6.3.3 (Mastering AMC 8)
complementary-counting.statement: How many three digit numbers contain the digit 5 at least once?

complementary-counting.name: Problem 6.3.4 (Mastering AMC 8)
complementary-counting.statement: How many 3 digit numbers have at least 2 digits that are the same?

complementary-counting.name: Problem 6.3.5 (Mastering AMC 8)
complementary-counting.statement: How many ways are there to select a group of 3 people from 5 women and 3 men if there must be at least 1 man in the group?

complementary-counting.name: Problem 6.3.7 (Mastering AMC 8)
complementary-counting.statement: How many 4 digit numbers are not palindromes? A palindrome is a number that reads the same forward and backward.

complementary-counting.name: Problem 6.3.11 (Mastering AMC 8)
complementary-counting.statement: There are 9 different flavors of ice cream at an ice cream shop. Joe wants to buy 2 or 3 scoops of different ice cream flavors. He doesn’t like 2 of the flavors Mango and Chocolate together so won’t buy them together. How many ways can he buy ice cre

complementary-counting.name: Problem 6.3.14 (Mastering AMC 8)
complementary-counting.statement: There are 10 slips of paper numbered 1 to 5 in a bag, each equally likely to be drawn. 87

complementary-counting.name: Problem 6.3.15 (Mastering AMC 8)
complementary-counting.statement: You have 6 identical pears and 5 identical apples. You choose 4 fruits at random. What is the probability of picking at least 1 pear? Answers 6.2 77 6.3 5 8 6.4 902 6.7 189/ 256 6.3.1 15 6.3.2 11 6.3.3 252 6.3.4 252 6.3.5 46 6.3.6 3 10 6.3.7 8910 6.3

inclusion-exclusion.name: Problem 7.5.1 (Mastering AMC 8)
inclusion-exclusion.statement: How many positive numbers less than or equal to 100 are multiples of 2 or 3?

inclusion-exclusion.name: Problem 7.5.2 (Mastering AMC 8)
inclusion-exclusion.statement: How many positive numbers less than or equal to 100 are NOT multiples of 2 or 3?

inclusion-exclusion.name: Problem 7.5.3 (Mastering AMC 8)
inclusion-exclusion.statement: Out of 200 students, there are 100 taking Geometry, 70 taking Algebra, and 30 tak- ing both. How many students are taking neither?

inclusion-exclusion.name: Problem 7.5.5 (Mastering AMC 8)
inclusion-exclusion.statement: In a town of 351 adults, every adult owns a car, motorcycle, or both. If 331 adults own cars and 45 adults own motorcycles, how many of the car owners do not own a motorcycle?

inclusion-exclusion.name: Problem 7.5.7 (Mastering AMC 8)
inclusion-exclusion.statement: How many positive numbers less than or equal to 200 are multiples of 2 and 3 but not a multiple of 5?

stars-and-bars.name: Problem 8.3.1 (Mastering AMC 8)
stars-and-bars.statement: How many ways are there to distribute 5 identical candies to 3 children?

stars-and-bars.name: Problem 8.3.6 (Mastering AMC 8)
stars-and-bars.statement: How many positive 5 digit integers exist such that each digit is greater than or equal to the previous digit?

geometric-counting.name: Problem 9.5.1 (Mastering AMC 8)
geometric-counting.statement: How many triangles can be formed by connecting 3 points in the figure below?

geometric-counting.name: Problem 9.5.2 (Mastering AMC 8)
geometric-counting.statement: How many squares of all sizes can be formed from a 3x3 grid of unit squares?

geometric-counting.name: Problem 9.5.3 (Mastering AMC 8)
geometric-counting.statement: How many rectangles of any size are in a 4 × 6 grid of squares?

geometric-counting.name: Problem 9.5.4 (Mastering AMC 8)
geometric-counting.statement: A mouse is standing on a grid at location (0,0). There is cheese on the grid at (5,5). He can only move right or up. How many different paths can he take to the cheese? Mouse Cheese

geometric-counting.name: Problem 9.5.8 (Mastering AMC 8)
geometric-counting.statement: How many rectangles of any size are in a 4 × 6 grid of squares?

geometric-counting.name: Problem 9.5.9 (Mastering AMC 8)
geometric-counting.statement: How many triangles can be formed by connecting 3 points in the figure below? 125

geometric-counting.name: Problem 9.5.11 (Mastering AMC 8)
geometric-counting.statement: Josh starts at the point (0,0) and needs to travel to his home at the point (6,7). There is a fire at the point (2,3) so he cannot travel there. How many ways are there for Josh to reach his home if he can only move up or right? Josh Home Fire

geometric-counting.name: Problem 9.5.12 (Mastering AMC 8)
geometric-counting.statement: Josh starts at the point (0,0) and needs to travel to his home at the point (6,7). There is a fire at the point (2,3) so he cannot travel there. Also, he needs to pick up a book from the library at the point (5,5). How many ways are there for Josh to

geometric-counting.name: Problem 9.5.13 (Mastering AMC 8)
geometric-counting.statement: Josh is now home at the point (6,7) but realizes he forgot something at school which is at the point (8,11). Because he is in a rush to get there before it closes, he is now willing to move diagonally (up-right) in addition to being able to go up and

geometric-counting.name: Problem 9.5.15 (Mastering AMC 8)
geometric-counting.statement: How many squares are in a 5x5 grid of squares with 2 diagonally opposite corners miss- ing? 128

geometric-counting.name: Problem 9.5.16 (Mastering AMC 8)
geometric-counting.statement: How many rectangles are in the figure below?

recursion-basics.name: Problem 10.4.1 (Mastering AMC 8)
recursion-basics.statement: Jeff is climbing a 7 step staircase. If he can climb 1 or 2 steps at a time, in how many ways can he get to the top? 135

recursion-basics.name: Problem 10.4.6 (Mastering AMC 8)
recursion-basics.statement: Jeff is climbing a 10 step staircase. However, there are spiders on the 3rd and 9th steps, and he refuses to step on those stairs. If he can climb 1 or 2 steps at a time, in how many different ways can he get to the top? Answers 10.2 26 10.4 7 27 10.

linear-equations-inequalities.name: Problem 12.4.5 (Mastering AMC 8)
linear-equations-inequalities.statement: I am thinking of 3 integers. Added two at a time their sums are 37, 41, 44. What is the product of the 3 integers?

linear-equations-inequalities.name: Problem 13.2.8 (Mastering AMC 8)
linear-equations-inequalities.statement: Joe drives at a speed of 30 miles per hour for 60 miles. He also drives at 45 miles per hour for 15 miles. Find the average speed of his car.

divisibility-primes.name: Problem 17.5.5 (Mastering AMC 8)
divisibility-primes.statement: The 7-digit numbers 74A52B1 and 326AB4C are each multiples of 3. Which of the following could be the value of C? (A) 1 (B) 2 (C) 3 (D) 5 (E) 8

divisibility-primes.name: Problem 17.5.15 (Mastering AMC 8)
divisibility-primes.statement: What is the prime factorization of 420? 227

divisibility-primes.name: Problem 17.5.16 (Mastering AMC 8)
divisibility-primes.statement: What is the prime factorization of 1001?

gcd-and-lcm.name: Problem 18.4.8 (Mastering AMC 8)
gcd-and-lcm.statement: How many positive factors does the number 60 have?

gcd-and-lcm.name: Problem 18.4.9 (Mastering AMC 8)
gcd-and-lcm.statement: Find the sum of factors of 320.

modular-arithmetic-intro.name: Problem 20.6.2 (Mastering AMC 8)
modular-arithmetic-intro.statement: What is the units digit of 1919 + 9999?

modular-arithmetic-intro.name: Problem 20.6.4 (Mastering AMC 8)
modular-arithmetic-intro.statement: What is the smallest number greater than 2 that leaves a remainder of 2 when divided by 3, 4, 5, or 6?

modular-arithmetic-intro.name: Problem 20.6.8 (Mastering AMC 8)
modular-arithmetic-intro.statement: What is the units digit of 1317 + 1713?

modular-arithmetic-intro.name: Problem 20.6.10 (Mastering AMC 8)
modular-arithmetic-intro.statement: Find the smallest positive number greater than 1 that leaves a remainder of 1 when divided by 2, by 3, by 4, by 5, and by 6.

linear-diophantine-equations.name: Problem 21.5.14 (Mastering AMC 8)
linear-diophantine-equations.statement: After the digits of a 3 digit number are reversed, the number is 297 more than the original number. What’s the largest possible value for the original number?

miscellaneous-number-theory.name: Problem 22.5.9 (Mastering AMC 8)
miscellaneous-number-theory.statement: Laura added two three-digit positive integers. All six digits in these numbers are differ- ent. Laura’s sum is a three-digit number S. What is the smallest possible value for the sum of the digits of S?

angle-chasing-parallel-lines.name: Problem 23.5.1 (Mastering AMC 8)
angle-chasing-parallel-lines.statement: In the adjoining figure, ABCD is a square, ABE is an equilateral triangle and point E is outside square ABCD . What is the measure of ∡AED in degrees? A BC D E

angle-chasing-parallel-lines.name: Problem 23.5.4 (Mastering AMC 8)
angle-chasing-parallel-lines.statement: In the figure, point A is the center of the circle, the measure of angle RAS is 74°, and the measure of angle RTB is 28°. What is the measure of minor arc BR, in degrees?

angle-chasing-parallel-lines.name: Problem 23.5.9 (Mastering AMC 8)
angle-chasing-parallel-lines.statement: In triangle ABC, AB = AC and ∠A = 40°. The bisector from ∠B intersects AC at point D. What is ∠BDC ?

triangle-fundamentals.name: Problem 24.6.14 (Mastering AMC 8)
triangle-fundamentals.statement: What is the radius of a circle inscribed in a triangle with side lengths 6, 8, and 10?
